_comment: Common locations where users keep git repositories on Windows. The defender module scans these roots for immediate-child directories that contain a .git folder and offers them as candidate Defender exclusions — but adding the PARENT dir (not each repo individually) to avoid the exclusion list ballooning as the user adds repos. Fields: id, path (env vars), description, defaultExclude (bool — if true, propose without asking; if false, always ask), reason.
_askUser: Always confirm with the user before adding any of these to Defender exclusions — a broad exclusion on Desktop or Documents removes real-time protection from a large portion of user files. Prefer excluding the tighter dev-tool subpaths in dev_cache_paths.json first, and only add these roots when the user explicitly wants to speed up building inside them.

roots.description: Convention used by the current user and by some indie dev communities — repos parked directly under Desktop\github.
roots.reason: Broad exclusion of an active project root. Ask user.

roots.description: Visual Studio's default 'Location' for new projects and Team Explorer clones.
User's Desktop root — scan for direct children with .git.
roots.reason: Way too broad to exclude wholesale — only enumerate for repo children.

roots.description: User's Documents root — scan for direct children with .git.
roots.reason: Way too broad to exclude wholesale — only enumerate for repo children.

roots.description: Common short-path convention (avoids long-path issues on nested Node/Rust projects).
roots.reason: If it exists, user chose this on purpose — likely benefits from exclusion. Ask.
